summary: `placementKey`'s NUL delimiter classes `sdk/src/declarations.ts` binary to grep/rg — swap it for U+001F so the seam file every row question routes through is searchable again

edit.description: `placementKey` (450-452) joins host/kind/key with two literal NUL bytes at 451, so grep/rg class the whole 729-line file as binary and print nothing — `grep -c placementKey` exits 1 on a file that defines the symbol. Swap both for U+001F (unit separator): the identical non-collision property over identifier components, and the key never leaves memory — `emit.ts` fills the map at 432 (importing the function at 34), `declarations.ts` reads it back at 492, and both `.temper/lock.toml` and `src/builtin_lock.toml` carry zero NUL bytes, so no delimiter round-trips through the lock. Both sides call the one function, so the swap is atomic by construction. The doc comment (445-449) names the identifying triple, not the delimiter — no narration change owed.

acceptance: `rg placementKey sdk/src/declarations.ts` prints its hits instead of nothing, so the rule's own instruction — `rg` both trees for the seam (.claude/rules/sdk.md, "The engine seam") — works on the file that same section names as the row-builder home. Behavior is unchanged: the key is an in-memory map key only, so the built-in lock re-derives byte-identically. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 inbox line (friction capture plan-declarations-ts-nul-byte-greps-blind.md, human-agreed, observed at 9862b2e). Diffed forward: 91c288c touched the file (715→729 lines), the NUL survives at 451. Swept every tracked .ts/.rs/.toml/.json/.md — declarations.ts is the only one carrying a NUL, so this entry is the whole class, not one instance. File-disjoint from every queued entry, hence open. scoped at b745415.

summary: Discovery classifies an adopted harness's matching files as its host's children through the host template's pattern — the child kind governing no glob to scan for

edit.description: `discover_builtin` (95-103) is the `governs`-keyed entry point, and 91c288c turned it into the explicit hole this entry fills: `match &kind.governs { Some(governs) => discover_kind_files(..), None => Ok(Vec::new()) }` — a nested-file kind has no `governs` to key on, so its members are discovered at none. Its units sit under each *host* unit at the host template's pattern, so the scan needs two facts one child kind cannot carry: which kind templates it (the host's `templates` column, `src/kind.rs`) and that host's own discovered units — name the signature this forces rather than fabricating a `governs` for the child. Extend the one scanner (`discover_kind_units`, 150, whose doc names it as serving every kind's locus) rather than adding a second walk beside it (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home"); the ignore-honoring `discoverable_paths` set (255) governs the child walk exactly as it governs a host's. Rider: two doc comments here credit a caller that does not exist — 91 ("`pub(crate)` so drift re-scans the harness") and 147-149 ("`pub(crate)` so the drift engine re-runs the same `governs`-keyed scan against a live harness"). No `src/drift.rs` caller is on disk; the crate-internal callers are `main.rs` and this module. Reconcile both while here.

`kind_features`'s unit dispatch (1139-1143) carries the matching hole and states it: `(_, _, None) => Vec::new()`, commented "A nested file kind is discovered off its host's template, never a `governs` walk — a locus it does not have — so no file surfaces for it here." That arm is what this entry replaces. `builtin_features_by_kind` (1295-1308) loops `builtin_defs.values()` per kind, so the ordering constraint lands here: a nested-file kind's members reach `by_kind` through their host's units, and the host's discovery must run before its children's.

`discover` (316-322) — the discovery report the `per` section opens with — loops every built-in kind through `import::discover_builtin`, so a nested-file kind contributes nothing to `DiscoveryReport::members` and its files never convert into member modules on the yes-path. This is the adopted-harness surface the acceptance names; without it the entry's claim is false for the verb that adopts.

`Template::path`'s doc (578-580) states the declared-fact-only bound TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT (794678f) shipped under: "Declared only: nothing discovers a file child off it, exactly as a host's embedded members resolve independently of its templates." That sentence is exactly what this entry retires; the doc moves with the behavior.

Discovery coverage for the file child, beside the projection/round-trip proofs 91c288c added (258, 307, 345): an adopted harness's file matching a host template's pattern classifies as that host's child, of the template's kind.

tests.asserts: a file under a host's unit matching the host template's path pattern is discovered as that host's nested file child, of the template's declared kind
a file under a host's unit that the pattern does not match is discovered as no member — unmodeled, never mis-classified

acceptance: Discovery classifies an adopted harness's matching files as their host's children through the host template's pattern (representation.md, "locus"; the pattern is the host's declared fact) — with no glob of the child kind's own scanned for, and the install discovery report counting them. `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 2. Gate OPENED: both blockers shipped at b745415. Rescoped against 91c288c: `src/drift.rs` DROPPED — it runs no `governs` scan (no caller on disk; it diffs the payload's owned set against the prior lock's rows), so the old "drift re-runs the scan" claim was false. `install.rs` + `kind.rs` added; 91c288c turned both holes into explicit commented arms. TEMPLATE-FILE-CHILD-FACT's declared-fact-only bound retires here. rescoped at b745415.

summary: The node-set and edge-scope predicates move onto the selection algebra: count/unique/membership/degree/kind judge over any declared selection, not only a requirement's satisfier set

edit.description: The five predicates 9f3541c fenced out of the per-artifact facet gain the judge the fence stands in for: `contract.md`, "selection" states one algebra over selections with the quantifier as the clause's grain — no separate universal/existential machinery — so `Count`/`Unique`/`Membership` are whole-grain over any selection and `Degree`/`Kind` are each-grain over it. The fence is `judgeless` (144-172), which returns the "ranges over a requirement's satisfier set, which a per-artifact contract clause has no judge for" message for the five under `Facet::Contract` (112-119); its `decide` counterpart is the defensive-floor arm at 537-542. Each fence entry retires as its predicate gains a judge, and `Facet` itself outlives the entry only if some predicate is still facet-split — `dependency-exists` is (judge-less in both), so the split stands. `vacuities` (177+) is untouched: an empty `kind` and an inverted bound are vacuous in every facet. `kind_violation` (553) is the existing each-grain entry point, pulled out because a satisfier's actual kind is roster context — the generalization is what lets it rejoin `decide`, or states why it cannot. Rider: the comment at 528-541 names this work as `REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT`, a tag no queue carries — reconcile the narration to what lands.

`roster` judges these five today over a requirement's satisfier set — the *only* selection they reach. The opt-in selection (`contract.md`, "selection": the members whose satisfies edge targets a requirement) is one selector among three, so this judging is the algebra's existential instance, never its home. Route it through the generalized judge rather than leaving a second implementation beside it (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").

The reference-graph context `Degree` needs — the context `engine::judgeless` (152-155) names as the reason the per-artifact facet fences it. A by-kind or by-incidence selection needs the same context a requirement's satisfier scan has, so the graph reaches the judge for every selection, not one selector. Rider (open-questions): the narration cites at 61 and 689 are dated @07-02 — the facts hold, the dates are stale; re-verify against the live docs and bump them while here.

The clause's selection binding: the five carry their selection today only implicitly, via the requirement facet that hosts them. The `Predicate` enum (the closed vocabulary) does not grow — adding one is a deliberate language change and this is not one; what moves is which selections a clause may bind to. Rider (open-questions): the retired-`effective` straggler at 490 ("when `target` (above) names a field for layering purposes") keeps the retired severity-flip layer's vocabulary — reconcile it while here.

The author-facing spelling of the five (`count`/`unique`/`membership`/`degree`/`kind`) — sayable outside a requirement's `clauses` array once the engine judges them there, so the SDK type surface stops implying the requirement facet is their only home.
The existing requirement-facet coverage: the five keep judging a requirement's satisfier set identically — the generalization adds selections, never moves the one that works.

`a_kind_clause_is_inadmissible_on_a_per_artifact_contract` (262, rewritten by 9f3541c) pins the fence this entry retires: a named `kind` on a kind's own contract inverts back to admissible once the judge lands, while an empty `kind` stays inadmissible on vacuity — the half `vacuities` keeps. The engine.rs test `a_predicate_this_facet_cannot_judge_is_inadmissible_as_a_contract_clause` (981+) and its `judgeless_predicates` fixture (957) move the same way.

`a_kind_contract_naming_a_judge_less_predicate_fails_loud_at_admissibility` (188, added 9f3541c): a `count` floor clause on `skill` fails the run today because the engine has no judge for it. Once it judges the by-kind selection, the fixture's clause is admissible and the assertion is what the entry inverts — the fail-loud bar itself stands, since `dependency-exists` is still judge-less in both facets and is the honest fixture for it. 5c34ced's embedded-kind dispatch proofs (276, 310) sit below and are untouched.

tests.asserts: count/unique/membership are whole-grain over a by-kind selection, and degree/kind each-grain over it — the same verdicts the requirement facet reaches over its opt-in selection
a predicate with no judge in any facet still fails loud at admissibility — the fence narrows to `dependency-exists`, it does not lapse
a requirement's satisfier-set clauses are unchanged — the opt-in selection is one selector, not a second machinery

acceptance: count/unique/membership/degree/kind judge over any declared selection — by kind, by opt-in, by incidence — with the quantifier the clause's grain and no separate universal/existential machinery (contract.md, "selection"), and no per-artifact clause among them is inadmissible for want of a judge. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 inbox ruling, entry 2. No decision owed: the vocabulary does not grow — code lags explicit spec text (`contract.md`, "selection"); 9f3541c's fence is the placeholder it retires, and that commit's own comments name this work REQUIREMENT-CLAUSES-RECUT. Gate OPENED: EMBEDDED-KIND-CONFORMANCE-DISPATCH shipped (5c34ced, b745415). Every cited line re-verified on disk; 5c34ced added to gate_fail_loud.rs below the fixture. File-disjoint from both open entries. rescoped at b745415.

summary: The built-in adoption: `skill` templates its bundled reference documents at the directory's markdown, and `supporting-doc` ships as their prose-only kind

edit.description: `skill` (142-158) declares no template. Give it its bundled reference documents as one file-child layer — `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16). New `supporting-doc` kind beside it: fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename, nested-file locus. Its default contract ships **empty** per the standing bar ("Default contracts"); its one clause is SUPPORTING-DOC-REACH-CLAUSE's to add once an incidence clause outside a requirement is admissible, never this entry's to invent. The kind ships without joining the surface enumeration, the `requirement` precedent. Rider (open-questions): the doc-comment cites at 565/611 name two deleted `packages/{rule,memory}.anthropic/PACKAGE.md` files — discharge them while here.

The engine-side reader mirror of the shipped kinds (the `CustomKind::new` calls and `definitions`): `skill`'s row grows its template, and `supporting-doc` enters as the child kind a discovered document is read as — 91c288c landed the `CustomKind::nested_file` constructor (src/kind.rs:600) this row is the first built-in caller of. Rider (open-questions): the narration cites here are dated @07-07 / @07-10 or undated (85, 194/222, 63/106) — every fact still holds; re-verify against the live docs and bump the dates while here.

The embedded built-in lock re-derives once `skill`'s row carries a template and `supporting-doc` has a row — the first built-in to declare either (91c288c left this file byte-identical because no built-in declared the locus; that changes here).
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the two grown rows.
`skill`'s declaration row carries its reference-document template, and `supporting-doc`'s row carries its nested-file locus and empty field schema.

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `skill`'s kind row grows its `templates` column (child kind `supporting-doc`, the markdown path pattern), and `supporting-doc` enters as a new kind row at the nested-file locus.

tests.asserts: `skill` declares one file-child layer of `supporting-doc` at the directory's markdown pattern, and `supporting-doc` is fields-free, prose-only, channel-less, identity from the filename
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically from the SDK with the two grown rows
a real skill directory's markdown document is discovered as that skill's `supporting-doc` child

acceptance: A skill's bundled reference documents are nested file members of the same skill, typed `supporting-doc` (builtins.md, "The shipped kinds"), their path the skill template's declared fact — nesting-is-model-containment / locus-is-serialization stops being an unbacked claim. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes 0027 (abe5d5d), Consequences half 3 — the built-in adoption; 0027 says it re-enters buildable, and it does, minus the clause. The template claims the honest markdown subset: supporting files of other types stay unmodeled and named as such (0027's second Rejected bullet), the `settings.json` partial-governance posture. Discharges the `supportingDocs()` factory asymmetry in open-questions. Gate re-tested at b745415: NESTED-FILE-DISCOVERY unshipped. scoped at 4ba483f.

summary: The fixture-harness wiring builder belongs in `tests/common`, not copy-pasted per suite: three copies of tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk` collapse into one home

edit.description: The declared one home for test scaffolding — its own header (1-4) names "the SDK vendoring used by tests that drive a real `node` subprocess" as the copy-per-suite it exists to end. It carries every ingredient (`tmpdir` 31, `scaffold` 44, `vendor_sdk` 96) and not the builder that composes them into a fixture harness, which is exactly the surface the three copies below each re-derive. Add that builder: a label plus the fixture program text, returning `(harness, into)`. `tests/install.rs`'s `vendor_sdk` calls are a different job — the install verb scaffolds its own `harness.ts` under test, rather than wiring an authored fixture program — and are out of scope.

`wire_sdk_harness_program` (1223) is the original, and the surface the other two copies' doc comments cite by name as the pattern they follow; `wire_sdk_harness` (1217) binds it to this suite's `HARNESS_PROGRAM`. Retire the local body onto the common home — the `HARNESS_PROGRAM` binding stays suite-local, being this suite's fixture rather than scaffolding.

`wire_memberless_harness` (61-71): a verbatim copy of emit.rs's builder — tmpdir, `.temper`, write `harness.ts`, `vendor_sdk` — whose own doc (58-60) cites `tests/emit.rs`'s `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it follows. Call the common builder with `MEMBERLESS_BUILTIN_PROGRAM`.

`wire_waypoint_harness` (141, shifted +24 by 91c288c's nested-file-child seam case), the third copy (e76ec85), carrying the same doc cite back to emit.rs's builder. Call the common builder with `WAYPOINT_PROGRAM`.

acceptance: One builder composes tmpdir → `.temper` → `harness.ts` → `vendor_sdk`, in `tests/common`, and no suite re-derives it (engineering.md, "One job, one home": shared fixtures and builders live in one home, never copy-pasted per file). Behavior is unchanged, so the three suites' existing tests are the coverage; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found sweeping 74f4e62..ca4e866: e76ec85 added the third copy, its doc citing emit.rs's `wire_sdk_harness` as the pattern it followed rather than the home it should have extended — the copy-per-suite `tests/common`'s own header says it exists to end. Serialized behind the chain: shares projection_path_seam.rs with the nested-file work, builtin_lock_frozen.rs with SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS. Re-verified at b745415: all three copies live. rescoped at b745415.

summary: `supporting-doc`'s reach clause: a supporting file is invisible unless its host skill references it — the by-kind universal binding, each-grain incidence, advisory

edit.description: `supporting-doc`'s default contract — the empty exported clause array SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS ships — gains its one clause: an incoming-degree bound over the kind's members, cited to code.claude.com/docs/en/skills "Add supporting files" (retrieved 2026-07-16), the vendor fact that every supporting file is invisible unless SKILL.md references it. **Universal, not existential**: the fact holds of every supporting document, so the spelling is the by-kind binding with each-grain incidence (`contract.md`, "selection"), never a requirement — a requirement is the opt-in selector and would make a vendor fact consumer ceremony. **Reach is locus-agnostic**: any resolved edge from the host skill counts, post-0025's one edge noun and one degree semantics — a mention included, this clause being the "a contract may" that mention's obligation-free default reserves (`contract.md`, "edge"). Severity **advisory**, per `intent.md` invariant 5: a shipped coverage clause enters advisory, and escalation is the corpus's declared act.
The embedded built-in lock re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains its one clause row.
The frozen gate re-derives the built-in lock through the real SDK and byte-compares; it moves with the grown clause row.
`supporting-doc`'s default contract carries exactly one clause, at advisory severity, cited and guided — the shipped-clause bar (`builtins.md`, "The clauses live in code").

schemaDelta: `src/builtin_lock.toml` re-derives: `supporting-doc`'s kind row gains one clause row (the incoming-degree reach bound, advisory).

tests.asserts: `supporting-doc`'s default contract is one advisory reach clause, cited to the vendor fact and carrying its guidance
the embedded built-in lock re-derives byte-identically with the clause row

acceptance: A supporting document no edge from its host skill reaches is an advisory finding naming the vendor fact (builtins.md, "Default contracts"; the clause is decidable, severity-declared, guided, cited and dated) — and reach counts any resolved edge, locus-agnostic. `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Routes the 07-16 ruling's clause itself (25e9317 resolved the fork). The single-tag gate names PREDICATE-SELECTION-ALGEBRA — without it an incidence clause outside a requirement is inadmissible, so the clause cannot ship. It ALSO requires SKILL-NESTED-REFERENCE-DOCS: that entry mints the kind and its empty contract, and shares builtins.ts / builtin_lock.toml / builtin_lock_frozen.rs — so this is the queue tail, behind both chains. scoped at d572827.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at b745415: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml:7-9 still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
